Introduction.p1: Hello everyone, thank you for reading my post. My name is <bold>Nguyen Ha Vy</bold>.
Introduction.p2: I graduated from a university of Economics in 2025. At the time of writing this, it is 2026, and I am working an office job unrelated to the IT field. My daily tasks mainly involve data entry, compiling data from Excel, and processing documents.
Introduction.p3: However, after working for a while, I realized that my current job does not offer many long-term growth opportunities. Additionally, the company has been trending toward downsizing, which made me start thinking more seriously about a new career path.
Introduction.p4: After doing some research, I decided to pursue a career as a <bold>Tester</bold> in the IT field. However, I must admit that my starting point is quite low:
Introduction.li2: I don't know anyone in the industry who could give me advice or guidance.
Introduction.li3: And at this point, I have virtually no knowledge about IT in general and Testing in particular.
Introduction.p5: Previously, I posted a few articles in the <link><bold>Testing VN</bold></link> group on Facebook to ask for advice. I received both encouragement and practical, thoughtful feedback from people in the industry. Those opinions left me somewhat uncertain, but they also helped me understand that the road ahead would not be easy.
Introduction.p6: Right now, what I can do is start with my own effort. Due to limited finances, I cannot yet enroll in paid courses, so my plan is to self-study in the evenings and on weekends to gradually learn and build a foundation of knowledge in Testing.
Introduction.p7: I wrote this post hoping to receive more advice, shared experiences, or learning guidance from those who are currently working in the industry. Any input would be incredibly valuable to me at this point.
Introduction.p8: I created this site to record my entire learning journey and career transition into Testing, like a study journal to track my progress over time.
Introduction.p9: Thank you everyone for taking the time to read this.
